title: Filtered latent wear state from a race-local state-space model
summary: Recent F1 work models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets and fuel adjustment, outperforming a simpler AR benchmark.
rationale: Recent F1 work models tyre degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets and fuel adjustment, outperforming a simpler AR benchmark. That suggests the current feature set may be missing a hidden wear signal that is better represented by filtered state, innovation, and state uncertainty than by raw `TyreLife` and `Cumulative_Degradation` alone.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race sequence, fit a lightweight causal state-space or Kalman-style model on past laps only using lap time, lap delta, compound, and estimated fuel burn; export filtered latent degradation level, one-step forecast error, and posterior variance as features for the downstream classifier.
expected_effect: Adds a dynamic ‘cliff’ indicator that can separate normal long stints from laps where pace loss is becoming strategically actionable.
risk: Using smoothed states or future observations would leak; only filtered online states should be used.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
